setup.title: Setup:
setup.consentMessage: Accept the participation consent to continue.
setup.profileMessage: Complete your research profile to start generating recommendations.

auth.heroSubtitle: Sign in with your email. One-time codes and run notifications are delivered via WhatsApp when enabled.
auth.feature1: Evidence-backed research ideas from real papers
auth.feature2: Personalized retrieval that learns from your feedback
auth.feature3: Secure sign-in via WhatsApp one-time codes
auth.whatsappHint: Include country code. Used for sign-in codes and optional run alerts.
auth.whatsappOptIn: Send OTP and run notifications via WhatsApp
auth.signInHint: Enter the email linked to your account. We send a one-time code to your saved WhatsApp number when notifications are enabled.
auth.withContactDetails: with your name, email, and WhatsApp number.
auth.changePhoneLater: You can change your phone or WhatsApp preferences later in Profile.

consent.pageSubtitle: Please read and accept before using POST-Rec. You won't see this again — it's always available in your profile.
consent.intro: POST-Rec is an experimental research tool. By continuing you acknowledge:
consent.panelIntro: POST-Rec is an experimental research tool. By participating you acknowledge that:
summary: Recommendations are generated by AI and may be incomplete or incorrect
All outputs require human review before any academic use
You may stop participating at any time
checkboxes: I accept to participate in this exploratory study.
I understand the system is experimental.
I understand recommendations must be reviewed by a human.
I understand I may withdraw at any time.

profile.title: Your profile

profile.goalLabel: What do you want from POST-Rec?

profile.preferencesIntro: These defaults pre-fill the new run form. You can still adjust them for each run.
profile.preferencesSaved: Recommendation preferences saved. New runs will use these defaults.

preferences.seedTopicsPlaceholder: One topic per line (spaces allowed within a line)
preferences.seedTopicsHint: Pre-filled when you start a new run. Press Enter for a new topic; phrases can include spaces.
preferences.defaultExpectedOutput: Ideas that can become a real paper using public datasets.
preferences.maxArticleAgeHint: Only retrieve papers published within this window.

newRun.title: New run
newRun.subtitle: Define topics for this run. Defaults come from your profile — adjust as needed.
newRun.seedTopicRequired: Add at least one seed topic.
newRun.learnedTopicsNote: Your profile includes {{count}} learned topic(s) from past feedback.

quick.label: Quick
quick.hint: Single-pass generation with enhanced SOTA fields and novelty verification.

sota.label: SOTA-grounded
sota.hint: Full landscape → gap matrix → proposals with strict critic filtering.

exploratory.label: Exploratory
exploratory.hint: Full multi-stage pipeline with higher weight on novelty and broader ideas.

fggv.label: FGGV (Facet-Grounded)
fggv.hint: Novel method: facet-level novelty verification, gap alignment, and diversity selection.

runs.title: Runs
runs.subtitleEmpty: Start a run to generate research ideas backed by real papers.

runs.noRunsDescription: Create your first run and we'll search papers, analyze gaps, and suggest ideas you can turn into research.
runs.completedWithoutIdeas: This run finished but no recommendations were saved. You can start a new run with different topics.

sotaVerification.title: SOTA verification

evidence.noArticles: No supporting articles cited for this recommendation.

survey.subtitle: Help us improve POST-Rec with a short closing survey.

survey.thankYou: Thank you — your feedback was recorded.

progress.recoverableHint: Temporary warnings are normal while sources are searched. The run continues automatically.

usage.title: Run usage
usage.estimatedNote: Estimated Gemini API cost based on tokens used.
usage.sharedGenerationNote: Ideas are generated in one shared LLM call, so per-idea cost is an average estimate.

transparency.title: How POST-Rec works
transparency.subtitle: A formal description of retrieval, scoring, generation, and feedback — so you know exactly how recommendations are produced.

overview.p1: POST-Rec is a literature-grounded research recommender. Each run combines open bibliographic sources, deterministic relevance filtering, and a large language model (LLM) constrained to cite only retrieved papers.
overview.bullet1: Dual-pass retrieval, BM25 + dense + pgvector hybrid ranking
overview.bullet2: SOTA-grounded multi-stage generation with deterministic verification
overview.bullet3: Verified ranking (SOTA fit + novelty_verified) with feedback calibration
overview.bullet4: Closed-loop learning from your ratings (Expectation Alignment Score)

pipeline.intro: Every recommendation run follows the same ordered pipeline. Progress events you see in the UI map to these stages.

expectation.title: Expectation & run creation
expectation.body: Your seed topics, research area, depth, run mode, and constraints are stored as a UserExpectation and linked to the run.

retrieval.title: Dual-pass multi-source search
retrieval.body: Expanded queries are sent to OpenAlex, arXiv, Semantic Scholar, and Crossref in SOTA (recent) and foundation (highly cited) passes.

relevance.title: Relevance filtering
relevance.body: Each paper receives a deterministic relevance score; papers below the threshold are discarded.

dedup.title: Normalization & deduplication
dedup.body: Titles, DOIs, and content hashes are normalized; duplicate documents are merged. Abstract limitations and method hints are tagged.

embedding.title: Embedding
embedding.body: Surviving papers are embedded with the configured Gemini embedding model and stored for hybrid re-ranking.

hybridRank.title: Hybrid re-ranking
hybridRank.body: Papers are re-scored with BM25 + dense cosine similarity + pgvector search, then tier quotas enforce a SOTA-recent mix.

sotaPipeline.title: SOTA pipeline generation
sotaPipeline.body: Quick mode uses enhanced single-pass generation; SOTA/Exploratory modes run landscape → gap matrix → proposals → critic.

verification.title: Novelty & citation verification
verification.body: Each idea is checked for citation grounding, SOTA anchors, embedding distance, and recency gap before publication.

validation.title: Validation & refinement
validation.body: Ideas passing validation are published; others are saved with needs_refinement status and shown separately.

ranking.title: Verified ranking & display
ranking.body: Published ideas are sorted by verified final_score and shown with SOTA verification metrics.

inputs.seedTopics: Seed topics — primary search phrases you provide
inputs.expectedOutput: Expected output — e.g. conference-ready ideas, survey angles
inputs.depth: Desired depth — quick vs. exhaustive exploration
inputs.constraints: Constraints — e.g. avoid real-user experiments, paper limits
inputs.profile: If you have a research profile, learned topics and preferred techniques expand the query token set:
inputs.expansionCaption: Expanded topic set used for retrieval

retrieval.p1: POST-Rec queries these open catalogs (no paywalled corpora):
retrieval.p2: Results are merged, normalized to a common schema (title, abstract, DOI, year, citations), then scored. Only the top-ranked papers proceed to generation.
retrieval.dualPass: Each query runs two retrieval passes: a SOTA pass (recent papers, recency-sorted) and a foundation pass (highly cited classics). Results are merged with a configurable SOTA quota.
retrieval.hybridNote: After embedding, papers are re-ranked with BM25 + dense cosine similarity + pgvector search, then tier quotas enforce ~{{quota}} recent SOTA papers in the final context.

relevance.intro: Before any LLM call, each paper p is scored by lexical overlap between your query tokens Q and the paper text.
relevance.overlapCaption: Token overlap ratio (stopwords removed, tokens length > 2)
relevance.tokenNote: Q is built from seed topics ∪ research area ∪ learned topics; avoided topics add a penalty when they overlap the paper body.
relevance.ifAvoidedOverlap: when avoided-topic tokens overlap the paper body
relevance.filter: Papers with s_rel < {{min}} are filtered out before generation.
relevance.weakCap: If both title and body overlap are very low, the score is capped at {{cap}} regardless of citation boost.

embeddings.p1: Each retained document d is embedded from its title and abstract:
embeddings.p2: Embeddings are persisted and used for hybrid re-ranking (BM25 + dense + pgvector). Generation uses the top-ranked papers as structured context; SOTA/exploratory modes run a multi-stage landscape → gap → proposal → critic pipeline.

generation.p1: The LLM receives your expectation fields and up to 30 retrieved papers. System rules enforce:
generation.rule1: Evidence-only — no invented DOIs, authors, or papers
generation.rule2: Structured JSON output matching the recommendation schema
generation.rule3: Each idea must link evidence papers with a why_relevant explanation
generation.p2: Temperature is set for diverse but grounded proposals. Token usage and estimated cost are recorded per run.

ideaScores.intro: Each generated idea includes seven normalized dimensions s_i ∈ [0, 100], assigned by the LLM during structured generation:
ideaScores.llmNote: These dimension scores are model judgments grounded in the supplied evidence — not hand-written rules. The final rank uses the weighted formula below.

dimensionDesc.relevance: Alignment with your seed topics and research area
dimensionDesc.novelty: Distance from saturated lines of work in the evidence
dimensionDesc.evidence: Quality and directness of supporting papers
dimensionDesc.feasibility: Practical executability with public data and methods
dimensionDesc.trend: Fit with recent momentum in the literature
dimensionDesc.strategic_fit: Match with your stated goals and constraints

finalScore.intro: Published ideas are ranked with a verified score that blends LLM dimension estimates with deterministic SOTA-fit and novelty-verification signals. Weights vary by run mode (Quick, SOTA, Exploratory).
finalScore.verifiedNote: novelty_verified combines embedding distance, claim overlap, and optional LLM novelty. sota_fit measures engagement with recent anchors. Weights can be recalibrated from your feedback over time.
finalScore.legacyNote: Legacy LLM-only weights (used only when verified signals are unavailable):
finalScore.display: If the LLM omits final_score, the backend recomputes it from dimensions using the same weights (e.g. relevance at {{weight}}).
finalScore.sort: Recommendations are sorted by verified final_score descending in the UI and API.

feedback.intro: When you rate an idea, POST-Rec computes an Expectation Alignment Score (EAS) on a 1–5 scale:
feedback.wouldUse: The would-use-in-paper field maps to a numeric contribution W:
feedback.profileLoop: Positive feedback adds techniques and titles to your learned topics; negative feedback adds avoided topics — influencing future retrieval via Q and p_avoid.

limitations.l1: LLM outputs remain probabilistic, but SOTA/Exploratory modes use multi-stage artifacts and a critic pass to reduce variance.
limitations.l2: Dimension scores are blended with verified signals (novelty_verified, sota_fit); weights can be recalibrated from your feedback.
limitations.l3: Retrieval now uses dual-pass SOTA/foundation fetching, BM25, dense embeddings, and pgvector — but catalog coverage limits still apply.
limitations.l4: Citation boost is capped in relevance scoring; SOTA quotas prioritize recent papers in the final context.
limitations.l5: Estimated API costs are approximate; see the usage panel on completed runs for token-level detail.
